GenAiPlugin.description: This topic covers recommendations for a field rep who is interested in finding out which HCPs would be most impacted by an Aetna's change in how they cover a drug called Immunexis. Things that would be considered in which HCPs are recommended include the number of patients that HCP sees that are covered by Aetna, the historical time frame for the HCP and his or her staff to follow the new recommended guidelines and how long it takes for patients to get onto therapy pre-change and post-change.

genAiPluginInstructions.description:
Based on the recent Aetna Policy change, which HCPs would be most valuable to visit? When a user asks "Based on the recent Aetna Policy change, which HCPs would be most valuable to visit?" respond with markdown:
Hi Evan! Here are the HCPs in your territory that have the most patients in our patient support programs covered by Aetna:
1. Dr. Aaron Morita - high prescriber of Immunexis and large patient population covered by Aetna. Dr Morita is historically a slow adopter on new policy changes which has led to a 24% delay in TTT. Policy Update emails sent by marketing have gone unopened.
2. Dr. Lydia Jones - large Immunexis patient population covered by Aetna. Several recent patients submitted to the Immunexis Patient Support Program did not have sufficient documentation. High office staff turnover often leads to delayed uptake of new processes which in-office education.
3. Dr. Brenda Brown - top provider for Everlast, Inc which has recently switched employee coverage to Aetna. RA is very prevalent in region and for which Immunexis is first line treatment. Dr Brown is historically slow to abide by policy changes and often requires significant support.
Some recommended actions for these HCPs include: ✅ Ensure HCPs review their current patients on IMMUNEXIS and identify those who will require reauthorization. ✅ HCPs should prepare and submit the necessary documentation as early as possible to avoid interruptions in therapy. ✅ Ensure HCPs inform their patients about potential changes to their coverage and discuss any out-of-pocket cost implications. ✅ Educate HCPs and their staff on the new reauthorization process.
Would you like me to schedule visits with any of these options?

GenAiPlugin.masterLabel: HCP affected by Aetna Policy Change
GenAiPlugin.scope: Your job is to assist in recommending the HCPs that should be visited and sharing details on why it is important to do so with these specific HCPs.
